name: Fiesta Chicken
recipeIngredient: 2 Chicken breasts, Whole, Boneless and Skinless
1/2 cup Chicken Broth or White Wine
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the chicken breasts and split them in half. Cut each piece again in half, resulting in 8 pieces total.
2. Dip the chicken pieces in flour, shaking off excess.
3. Rinse the peppers, remove seeds and membranes, then slice.
4. Heat the butter in a skillet over medium heat until it begins to brown. Cook the chicken pieces on all sides, then sprinkle with garlic salt (if using), salt, and pepper. Remove from the skillet and set aside.
5. Add oil to the same skillet over medium heat. Place the peppers in the pan and cover with a lid. Cook for about 5 minutes, shaking the pan occasionally until they are tender.
6. Remove the peppers from the skillet.
7. Dissolve the chicken bouillon cube in hot water (about 1/2 cup).
8. Add the dissolved bouillon to the skillet. Stir to incorporate the pan juices and drippings.
9. Add the wine, followed by the chicken pieces and peppers back into the pan. Cover with a lid and cook for an additional 6 to 7 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through.
10. Sprinkle fresh herbs (such as parsley) over the dish, if available, or add thinly sliced green onions before serving.
Tips:
- You may add crumbled cooked bacon on top of the dish before serving.
- Alternatively, substitute garlic powder with 2 sliced green onions and sprinkle them over the finished dish.
